steps.title: Pin down the time relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1: in 2014 Gomez won his first storytelling competition. Sentence 2: in 2017 he created a show as a platform for others. The two sentences mark two distinct points in time, three years apart.

steps.title: Decide what the transition must do
steps.content: The blank needs to signal that the second event happened after the first — a chronological transition, not a contrast, example, or restatement.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Instead,': signals one thing replacing another, but creating the show didn't replace winning the competition. ✗
  • Choice B — 'For example,': signals an illustration of a prior claim, but the 2017 show is a later event, not an example of the 2014 win. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Later,': signals that the next event happened after the first, matching 2014 then 2017. ✓
  • Choice D — 'In other words,': signals a restatement, but the two sentences describe different events, not the same idea reworded. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
